Berlin, Germany · Postdoctoral Researcher

Barış Büyüktaş

I work on machine learning for Earth observation, building models that learn from large-scale satellite data to better understand the Earth's surface.

Postdoctoral researcher at BIFOLD & TU Berlin. My research interests include computer vision, foundation models, multimodal learning, and federated learning for remote sensing.

02

Selected publications

Journal Articles 04

J1

FedX: Explanation-guided pruning for communication-efficient federated learning in remote sensing

B. Büyüktaş, J. Klotz, B. Demir

IEEE J-STARS · vol. 19, pp. 8549–8560

Communication cost is a key bottleneck in federated learning for remote sensing. FedX addresses it by using model explanations to identify the most informative parameters and derive a mask matrix that prunes the aggregated global model — sharply reducing the data exchanged between clients and server while preserving classification accuracy.

arXivCode
2026
J2

A multi-modal federated learning framework for remote sensing image classification

B. Büyüktaş, G. Sumbul, B. Demir

IEEE TGRS · vol. 63, pp. 1–11

Real-world remote-sensing archives are distributed across institutions that hold different sensor modalities and cannot share raw data. This framework lets such clients collaboratively train a single model — aligning heterogeneous multi-modal representations across the federation without ever centralizing the imagery.

arXivCode
2025
J3

Federated learning across decentralized and unshared archives for remote sensing image classification: A review

B. Büyüktaş, G. Sumbul, B. Demir

IEEE GRSM · vol. 12, pp. 64–80

A comprehensive review of federated learning for remote-sensing image classification: it surveys methods for training across decentralized and unshared archives, organizes the field by data, model, and communication strategies, and outlines the open challenges and research directions for privacy-preserving Earth observation.

arXivCode
2024
J4

More learning with less labeling for face recognition

B. Büyüktaş, Ç. E. Erdem, T. Erdem

Elsevier Digital Signal Processing · vol. 136

Labeling face data at scale is expensive. This work combines semi-supervised and active-learning strategies so the model learns effective face representations from far fewer labeled examples — achieving higher recognition accuracy with a fraction of the annotation effort.

arXiv
2023

Conference Proceedings 04

C1

Communication-efficient federated learning based on explanation-guided pruning for remote sensing image classification

J. Klotz, B. Büyüktaş, B. Demir

IEEE IGARSS

The conference precursor to the FedX journal work: it introduces explanation-guided pruning to lower the communication overhead of federated learning for remote-sensing image classification, using interpretability signals to decide which model weights to transmit.

arXivCode
2025
C2

Transformer-based federated learning for multi-label remote sensing image classification

B. Büyüktaş, K. Weitzel, S. Völkers, F. Zailskas, B. Demir

IEEE IGARSS

Brings Vision Transformer backbones into a federated setting for multi-label remote-sensing classification, showing how attention-based architectures can be trained across decentralized clients while handling the multiple co-occurring land-cover labels typical of satellite scenes.

arXivCode
2024
C3

Learning across decentralized multi-modal remote sensing archives with federated learning

B. Büyüktaş, G. Sumbul, B. Demir

IEEE IGARSS

An early study on learning across decentralized, multi-modal remote-sensing archives: it demonstrates that federated learning can fuse knowledge from clients with different imaging modalities to train a shared classifier without ever pooling the underlying data.

arXivCode
2023
C4

Curriculum learning for face recognition

B. Büyüktaş, Ç. E. Erdem, T. Erdem

IEEE EUSIPCO

Applies curriculum learning to face recognition: by presenting training samples in a meaningful easy-to-hard order rather than randomly, the model converges to more discriminative face embeddings and improved recognition performance.

arXiv
2021
